g.text: WAL vs Rollback Journal: Inverted Strategies
journal must be fsynced BEFORE database is written
WAL must be fsynced BEFORE commit is acknowledged
nothing — database is never touched mid-tx
always go to database.db (may be mid-mutation)
union of database.db + WAL frames ≤ max_frame
WAL only — merges WAL frames back into database (background)
① Acquire EXCLUSIVE lock (block all readers)
② Copy OLD page → journal file
④ Write NEW data to database file

text.tspan: → UNDO: copy OLD pages back to database

g.text: ① Acquire WAL write lock (readers still run)
② Append NEW page frames → WAL file
③ Write commit frame to WAL
⑤ Database file untouched until checkpoint
⑥ COMMIT = commit frame visible in WAL

text.tspan: → REDO: replay committed frames into database

g.text: ✅ read database + WAL up to max_frame
